All States · Tennessee

Tennessee Dietitian License

Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners. $140 application. 4 to 6 Weeks processing.

Last verified 2026-02-25 · Board-sourced data
↗ Official Board Portal (opens in a new tab)

Quick Facts at a Glance

Application Fee
$140
$140 application fee (includes state regulatory fee)
Processing
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
Renewal
$90
Every 2 years (by birth month) — no CE required for state renewal
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Licensing Authority

Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners

Tennessee Department of Health
Phone
(615) 532-3202
Mailing Address
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243
Physical Address
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243
Credential Categories

Tennessee Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)

The standard credential required to practice dietetics or nutrition in Tennessee, regulated by the Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners under the Department of Health.

Temporary Permit

Available for applicants who have been approved by CDR to sit for the registration exam but have not yet passed it. Costs $5 and is valid until exam results are received. Requires a complete application on file.

Application Process

How to Get Licensed in Tennessee

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program in dietetics, human nutrition, or a related field. Complete at least 1,200 hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. The CDR exam fee is $200.

Submit the Online Application

Apply through the Tennessee Online Application system. Pay the non-refundable $140 application fee. Include a passport-style photo and declaration of citizenship form.

Complete Background Check & Provide Documentation

Authorize and complete a criminal background check. Submit official transcripts, proof of CDR registration, verification of supervised practice completion, and a letter of recommendation.

Required Documents

Application Checklist

  • ✓
    Official College Transcripts Sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.
  • ✓
    CDR Registration Card Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
  • ✓
    Supervised Practice Verification Documentation verifying completion of ACEND-accredited supervised practice hours.
  • ✓
    Passport-Style Photograph Recent photo meeting standard passport specifications.
  • ✓
    Criminal Background Check Authorization Mandatory background screening as part of the application process.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules
Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 to applicants who have completed all requirements except passing the CDR exam. The permit holder must have CDR authorization to test and a complete application on file with the Board. The permit is valid until exam results are received and does not require supervision.
Reciprocity & Endorsement
Tennessee does not offer formal licensure by endorsement or reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Tennessee license through the standard application process, which requires current CDR registration, a complete application, and a background check. Holding an active CDR credential satisfies the education and examination requirements.
Travel Dietitian Timeline
If your assignment start date is within 5 weeks, apply immediately and call the board at (615) 532-3202 to confirm current processing times.

Note: Tennessee does not offer a standard provisional license, so your license must be issued before your first day.
Maintenance

Renewal Requirements

Tennessee dietitian licenses renew every 2 years (by birth month) — no ce required for state renewal. Renewal fee is $90.

Frequently Asked Questions

Tennessee Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Tennessee dietitian license cost?

The initial application fee is $140, which includes the state regulatory fee. The CDR exam costs an additional $200. The biennial renewal fee is $90, due by the last day of your birth month.

Does Tennessee require continuing education for dietitians?

Tennessee does not mandate state-level continuing education for license renewal. However, to maintain your CDR registration — which is required for state licensure — you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years.

Can I practice in Tennessee while waiting for my license?

Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 for applicants who have CDR authorization to test. This permit allows practice while you await your exam results, provided your application is complete and on file with the Board.
